name: Robert's Central Laupahoehoe
principals: Iwamoto family (historic founders; Robert Iwamoto Sr.)
William Baccaro (local manager per source data)
districts_or_customers: Hawaii DOE (private school routes on Oahu)
recent_news: Name in the source list appears to be a data-aggregation artifact combining three historic Hawaii school bus operators (Roberts Hawaii School Bus, Central Transportation, Laupahoehoe Transportation) that consolidated/competed under DAGS contracts in the 1990s; surviving operator is Roberts Hawaii, now an ESOP with ~900 vehicles and ~1,400 employees (per 2024-2025 company/Wikipedia data).
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Roberts Hawaii is the dominant Hawaii statewide bus/tour operator, employee-owned via ESOP, and would be a transformational platform-scale acquisition rather than a tuck-in. Hawaii is also geographically disconnected from Summit's mainland operating footprint.

name: Roberts Student Transportation
districts_or_customers: Hawaii DOE / Big Island public school routes (Kona complexes)
recent_news: Address (74-5610 Alapa St / 73-4800 Kanalani St, Kailua-Kona) and phone (808-326-1190) match Roberts Hawaii's Kona school bus yard; this is a branch/division of Roberts Hawaii, not an independent operator. Roberts Hawaii is ESOP-owned with ~900 vehicles statewide.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. This is a branch of Roberts Hawaii (ESOP), not an independent operator. Phone number matches Roberts Hawaii's Kona school bus desk. Hawaii also outside Summit's mainland geographic focus.

name: Akita Enterprises LTD
districts_or_customers: Hawaii DOE - Kapaa and Kauai school complexes (awarded 2017-18 contracts)
also services Hawaii, Maui, Lanai on regular & SPED contracts
recent_news: In 2017, Akita Enterprises was awarded new Hawaii DOE contracts covering the Kapaa and Kauai complexes as part of the state's third-phase transportation reform; company has operated continuously since 1946 as a family business led by President Wendy Akita.
fit_notes: Genuine independent family-owned Kauai school bus contractor with multi-island DOE work and 75+ years of operating history - profile fits the tuck-in archetype. However, Hawaii is outside Summit's mainland focus geography; succession question is open given long-tenured family ownership. Worth a watchlist note if Summit ever pursues Pacific expansion.

name: Freedom II Limousine Svc LLC
service_types: school/college transportation (secondary; some classification under school & employee bus transportation NAICS)
recent_news: Established 2016 as a DC LLC; WMATC (Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Commission) regulatory orders list Ronald Payton as CEO. Primarily luxury black car/limousine services in the DMV, with school/college transportation listed as a service category rather than as a meaningful yellow-bus school contract.
website: https://www.freedomiilimoservices.com/ (per Yellow Pages listing; not independently confirmed)
fit_notes: Poor fit. This is primarily a limousine/black-car operator (not a yellow-bus school district contractor). Small employee count, no evidence of meaningful school district or SPED contracts. Likely miscoded into the school bus universe via secondary NAICS. Pass.

name: G & D Bus Svc
service_types: bus service (unspecified; likely small charter or contract)
recent_news: No web footprint identified for a 'G & D Bus' operating at 915 52nd St NE, Washington DC; no website, no DCPS/OSSE contract evidence, no press coverage. DC special-education student transport is largely run in-house by OSSE-DOT, which limits the independent yellow-bus contractor opportunity in the District.
fit_notes: Very low confidence target. No discoverable web presence, lead exec, or contract record. Could be a small charter/transport operator with negligible school exposure, or could be inactive/dissolved. Recommend deprioritizing until DOT/SAFER, DC corporate registry, or phone outreach can confirm it is an operating school bus contractor.
